notes: 2. Can anyone be righteous before God? (25:4-6) a. No one can be pure in God's sight b. If the moon and stars pale in God's sight, how much more man, who is no more than a maggot or worm in comparison to God
BILDAD'S FINAL RESPONSE (25:1-6) 1. He proclaims the greatness of God (25:1-3) a. Dominion and fear belong to Him, He makes peace in His high places b. His armies are innumerable

quotes.text: Job's Reply to Bildad's Third Speech Job impatiently breaks off Bildad's speech. He is just as aware of God's might as is Bildad, and can speak of it at least as eloquently. 26:5 Shades: the shadowy spirits of the dead. The underworld is sometimes imagined as being not merely under the earth but under the water that is beneath the earth (Ps. 136:6).
quotes.speaker: Raymond P. Scheindlin
quotes.attr: Raymond P. Scheindlin, The Book of Job, W. W. Norton & Company New York, 1998, pp 201
